turns.content: What brings you in today?

I've had a sore throat and a persistent cough for the past three or four days. It started with just a tickle in my throat, but now it's more of a raw sensation, and the cough is dry but occasionally productive in the mornings. My roommate was diagnosed with the flu last week, so I know I've been exposed, but I don't think I have a fever or body aches, so I figured it's worth a check-up just to be safe.
When did this start?

It started about four days ago, I think—Thursday evening, actually. I remember because I had a late study session and my throat felt a little scratchy by the end of it. By Friday morning, it was noticeably sore, and the cough started later that day.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

The soreness is mostly centered in my throat, around the back of my pharynx—it feels raw and slightly swollen, especially when I swallow. There's also a mild, scratchy irritation that extends up toward my soft palate, but no sharp or stabbing pain. It's more of a persistent discomfort than anything intense, maybe a 2 out of 5 in terms of severity.
Have you had any fever or chills?

I haven't taken my temperature, but I haven't felt feverish or had any chills. No night sweats or feeling overheated either, so I don't think I've had a fever. My roommate definitely had a high fever with the flu, so that's one difference I noticed.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not on any prescription medications right now. For the sore throat, I've been using over-the-counter throat lozenges with menthol and drinking warm honey-lemon tea, but nothing stronger. I don't take any daily pills or supplements either—just occasional ibuprofen if I have a headache, but I haven't needed that for this.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, I don't have any chronic medical conditions. I'm generally healthy—no asthma, diabetes, or autoimmune issues. I've never been hospitalized or had any major surgeries either.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I don't have any known drug allergies. I've taken antibiotics in the past without any issues, and over-the-counter medications like ibuprofen have always been fine for me.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Yes, I've been using menthol throat lozenges every few hours, and I've been gargling with warm salt water once or twice a day, which seems to help a little. I've also increased my fluid intake—mostly water and herbal tea—but I haven't tried any cough suppressants or decongestants yet.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Warm liquids like tea with honey definitely soothe my throat temporarily, but talking for extended periods or being in a dry environment makes the cough worse. Also, lying down seems to trigger more coughing fits, especially at night, so I've been propping myself up with an extra pillow.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been a bit disruptive, honestly. I've had trouble concentrating during lectures because of the constant throat irritation, and the coughing fits at night have been waking me up, so I'm more tired than usual. I've also been avoiding group study sessions or social gatherings, just to be cautious and not spread anything to my classmates.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?

No, I don't smoke at all—never have. And I only drink occasionally, maybe a glass of wine or a cocktail at social events once or twice a month, but not since my symptoms started. I figured it wouldn't help with the throat irritation.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

Not that I know of. My parents and siblings live in a different city, and they've all been healthy lately. As far as I'm aware, no one in my immediate family has had a sore throat or cough like this recently, so I think my exposure was mostly from my roommate.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

I actually had a similar sore throat and cough about two years ago, during my freshman year of college. That time, it was diagnosed as a viral pharyngitis and resolved on its own after about a week. The main difference then was that I had a low-grade fever, which I don't think I have now.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, that all makes sense. Your questions have been very thorough, and I appreciate that you're covering possible exposures, my symptoms in detail, and my medical history. It helps me feel like we're on the same page about what's going on.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say around a 4 out of 10 — the sore throat and cough are more annoying than truly painful, but the disrupted sleep is starting to wear me down. Emotionally, maybe a 3 out of 10 — I'm not really anxious or upset, just a bit tired and mildly frustrated that it's lingering longer than I expected.
Do you have any questions for me?

Yes, actually, I have a couple of questions. First, based on what I've described, do you think this is likely a viral infection like a common cold or something that might need antibiotics, like strep throat? And second, if it is viral, are there any over-the-counter treatments you'd recommend specifically for the cough, especially since it's keeping me up at night? I'm also curious if I should be concerned about the flu given my roommate had it, even though I don't have a fever.
What do you think might be going on?

I'm honestly leaning toward thinking it's a viral upper respiratory infection, maybe something like a common cold or a mild case of pharyngitis. Given that I don't have a fever or body aches, it probably isn't the same flu my roommate had, but I know some viral infections can present similarly. I'm also a little curious if strep throat is a possibility, since the soreness is pretty localized and raw, but I don't have swollen lymph nodes or white patches that I can see. What do you think based on your exam?
Is there anything else you think I should know?

Actually, I did want to mention that I've been under quite a bit of stress lately with final exams approaching, so my sleep has been irregular even before this cold started. I'm wondering if that might have made me more susceptible to catching whatever my roommate had. Other than that, I think I've covered everything—unless there's something specific you think I should be monitoring for, like if my symptoms change.
